# Sound Bath Calendar

> A curated, weekly-updated calendar of sound baths across Denver and the Front Range: Boulder, Fort Collins, and Colorado Springs. Dates, times, venues, prices, and ticket links for every listed session. Kept by Firstwater (thefirstwater.co).

## Pages

- [The calendar](https://soundbathcalendar.com/): Every sound bath on the Front Range, grouped by time — today, this weekend, this week, and the weeks ahead — with each listing tagged by city (Denver, Boulder, Fort Collins, Colorado Springs). Updated through the week.
- Every listed event has its own page at https://soundbathcalendar.com/event/<slug>/ with the date, time, venue, price, and the operator's ticket link. The full list of current event pages is in the sitemap: https://soundbathcalendar.com/sitemap.xml

## City pages

Each city page is the same time-grouped calendar filtered to one area, with its own summary, FAQ, and event list:

- [Denver](https://soundbathcalendar.com/denver/): Sound baths in Denver, grouped by time (today, this weekend, this week, the weeks ahead).
- [Boulder](https://soundbathcalendar.com/boulder/): Sound baths in Boulder, grouped by time.
- [Fort Collins](https://soundbathcalendar.com/fort-collins/): Sound baths in Fort Collins, grouped by time.
- [Colorado Springs](https://soundbathcalendar.com/colorado-springs/): Sound baths in Colorado Springs, grouped by time.

## Directories & discovery

- [Browse](https://soundbathcalendar.com/browse/): Every kind of session the calendar tracks — by modality, intention, setting, and access — with live counts. Categories with enough upcoming sessions have their own landing page (e.g. https://soundbathcalendar.com/gong-bath/).
- [Map](https://soundbathcalendar.com/map/): Every upcoming session pinned by venue on one map of the Front Range.
- [Practitioners](https://soundbathcalendar.com/practitioners/): Profiles of the facilitators who lead sessions on the calendar, each with a bio and upcoming dates.
- [Venues](https://soundbathcalendar.com/venues/): The rooms that host sound baths — address, what the space is like, and each venue's upcoming sessions.
- [Organizers](https://soundbathcalendar.com/operators/): The studios and collectives running the listed sessions, each with its upcoming dates.
- [Roundups](https://soundbathcalendar.com/roundups/): Editorial shortlists from the calendar's own data — e.g. rooms worth knowing — refreshed as the scene changes.
- [Learn](https://soundbathcalendar.com/learn/): The glossary and explainer index; the individual pages are listed under Learn below.

## Original data (free to cite)

- [State of Sound Healing on the Front Range](https://soundbathcalendar.com/state-of-sound-healing/): An original-data snapshot of the Front Range sound bath scene, derived from every session on the calendar and updated as a recurring report. The Summer 2026 edition: ~22 sessions per week across four metros, a $40 median ticket price, about one in three sessions free or by donation, and 71% starting after 5 p.m. Every figure is reproducible; the report is free to cite (please attribute "Sound Bath Calendar"). Dated editions are archived at https://soundbathcalendar.com/state-of-sound-healing/<edition>/.

## Learn (glossary & explainers, free to cite)

Plain-language reference pages — definitional answers first, no unsupported healing claims. Index: https://soundbathcalendar.com/learn/

- [What to expect at a sound bath](https://soundbathcalendar.com/what-to-expect/): The honest first-timer's guide — what happens, what to wear, cost, whether it is religious, and a careful non-medical health note.
- [What is a gong bath?](https://soundbathcalendar.com/what-is-a-gong-bath/): A sound bath where large gongs are the main instrument; loudness, format, and how it differs from bowl-led sessions.
- [Singing bowls vs. gongs](https://soundbathcalendar.com/singing-bowls-vs-gongs/): Steady focused tones vs. dense evolving waves; which is gentler for a first-timer.
- [What is a crystal bowl sound bath?](https://soundbathcalendar.com/what-is-a-crystal-bowl-sound-bath/): Quartz-glass singing bowls — "crystal" means the material, not gemstone healing.
- [432 Hz, explained](https://soundbathcalendar.com/432hz-explained/): An alternative tuning standard (A = 432 instead of 440). A real aesthetic preference; no solid evidence of unique healing effects.
- [What does the science actually say?](https://soundbathcalendar.com/sound-bath-science/): Small studies support short-term self-reported relaxation; claims of healing disease or cellular effects are unsupported.
- [Is a sound bath religious?](https://soundbathcalendar.com/is-a-sound-bath-religious/): Mostly no — most sessions ask nothing of your beliefs; some add ceremony, and the honest answer has more texture than a yes or a no. Written for people of faith and for skeptics.
- [Where sound baths come from](https://soundbathcalendar.com/where-sound-baths-come-from/): The real lineage — Asian gong traditions, India's nada yoga, and the largely modern "ancient Tibetan singing bowl" story.
- [Sound bath vs. meditation](https://soundbathcalendar.com/sound-bath-vs-meditation/): Receptive vs. active practice; why sound baths suit people who say they cannot meditate.

## Machine-readable endpoints

- [sitemap.xml](https://soundbathcalendar.com/sitemap.xml): Every indexable page, with per-page lastmod dates.
- [feed.xml](https://soundbathcalendar.com/feed.xml): RSS feed of upcoming sessions. Per-city feeds at https://soundbathcalendar.com/<city-slug>/feed.xml (denver, boulder, fort-collins, colorado-springs).
- [front-range.ics](https://soundbathcalendar.com/front-range.ics): Subscribable iCalendar feed of every upcoming session. Per-city calendars at https://soundbathcalendar.com/<city-slug>.ics, and each event page serves its own event.ics alongside it.
- Raw JSON under https://soundbathcalendar.com/data/: external-events.json (every listed session), sessions-cache.json (Firstwater's own sessions), practitioners.json / venues.json / operators.json (the curated directories), geocode.json (venue coordinates), and insights/summer-2026.json (the State of Sound Healing dataset).
